component.description: Full Featured N+1 and ORing Power Rail Controller

absolute_maximum_ratings.raw_name: A, C, FLTR, VDD, STAT voltage

absolute_maximum_ratings.raw_name: GATE short to A or C or GND

electrical_characteristics.conditions: Worst case, gate in active range
V(UV) rising, V(OV) = 0 V, PG goes high
V(OV) rising, V(UV) = 1 V, PG goes low
V(UV) = 0 V, V(OV) = 0 V, V(PG) = 0.4 V

electrical_characteristics.raw_name: UV / OV leakage current (source or sink)

electrical_characteristics.conditions: V(UV) = 1 V, V(OV) = 0 V, 0 <= V(PG) <= 5 V
V(FLTB) = 0.4 V, V(GATE)-A = 0 V, V(A-C) = 0.1 V
V(A) = V(C) + 20 mV, V(GATE-A) falling until FLTB switches low
V(A-C) = 0.1 V, increase V(A-C) until FLTB switches low

electrical_characteristics.parameter: TPS2410 forward turn-on and regulation voltage
electrical_characteristics.raw_name: TPS2410 forward turn-on and regulation voltage

electrical_characteristics.conditions: GATE sinks > 10 mA at V(GATE-A) = 2 V, V(A-C) falling, R(RSET) = open
GATE sinks > 10 mA at V(GATE-A) = 2 V, V(A-C) falling, R(RSET) = 28.7 kΩ
GATE sinks > 10 mA at V(GATE-A) = 2 V, V(A-C) falling, R(RSET) = 3.24 kΩ

electrical_characteristics.parameter: Additional threshold shift with STAT held low
electrical_characteristics.raw_name: Additional threshold shift with STAT held low

electrical_characteristics.conditions: V(A) = 12 V, V(A-C): 20 mV -> -20 mV, V(GATE-A) begins to decrease
V(A) = 12 V, C(GATE-GND) = 0.01 µF, V(A-C): 20 mV -> -20 mV, measure the period to V(GATE) = V(A)
V(A-C) = -0.1 V, V(C) = VDD, 3 <= VDD <= 18 V, 2 V <= V(GATE) <= 18 V

pin_definitions.description: Input power for the gate drive charge pump and internal controls. VDD must be connected to a supply voltage >= 3 V.
Connect a resistor to ground to program the turn-off threshold. Leaving RSET open results in a slightly positive V(A-C) turn-off threshold.

STAT is a multifunction pin. A high output indicates that the MOSFET gate is being driven high. Overdriving STAT low while GATE is high shifts the fast-turnoff threshold negative. STAT has a weak pull-up to VDD.
Open drain fault output. Fault is active (low) for any of the following conditions: Insufficient VDD, GATE should be high but is not, The MOSFET should be ON but the forward voltage exceeds 0.4 V.

OV is a voltage monitor that contributes to the PG output, and also causes the MOSFET to turn off if it is above the 0.6-V threshold. OV is programmable via an external resistor divider. An OV voltage above 0.6 V indicates a bus voltage that is too high.

UV is a voltage monitor that contributes to the PG output. The UV input has a 0.6 V threshold and is programmable via an external resistor divider. A UV voltage above 0.6V indicates a bus voltage that is above its minimum acceptable voltage. A low UV input does not effect the gate drive.
Connect to the gate of the external MOSFET. Controls the MOSFET to emulate a low forward-voltage diode.
This pin must be connected to GND.
Voltage sense input that connects to the simulated diode cathode. Connect to the MOSFET drain in the typical configuration.
Voltage sense input that connects to the simulated diode anode. A also serves as the reference for the charge-pump bias supply on BYP. Connect to the MOSFET source in the typical configuration.

A capacitor connected from FLTR to A filters the input to the fast comparator. Filtering allows the TPS2410 to ignore spurious transients on the A and C inputs. This pin may be left open to achieve the fastest response time.
Connect a storage capacitor from BYP to A to filter the gate drive supply voltage.
An open-drain Power Good indicator. PG is open if the UV input is above its threshold, the OV is below its threshold, and the internal UVLO is satisfied.

logical_pins.name: VDD
logical_pins.description: Input power for the gate drive charge pump and internal controls. Must be connected to a supply voltage ≥ 3 V.

logical_pins.name: RSET
logical_pins.description: Connect a resistor to ground to program the turn-off threshold.

logical_pins.name: STAT
logical_pins.description: Multifunction pin. High output indicates MOSFET gate is driven high. Overdriving STAT low shifts fast-turnoff threshold negative.

logical_pins.name: FLTB
logical_pins.description: Open drain fault output. Active low for insufficient VDD, gate drive failure, or excessive forward voltage.

logical_pins.name: OV
logical_pins.description: Overvoltage monitor input. Programmable via an external resistor divider.

logical_pins.name: UV
logical_pins.description: Undervoltage monitor input. Programmable via an external resistor divider.

logical_pins.name: GATE
logical_pins.description: Connect to the gate of the external MOSFET to emulate a low forward-voltage diode.

logical_pins.name: RSVD
logical_pins.description: Reserved pin. This pin must be connected to GND.

logical_pins.name: C
logical_pins.description: Voltage sense input that connects to the simulated diode cathode (MOSFET drain).

logical_pins.name: A
logical_pins.description: Voltage sense input that connects to the simulated diode anode (MOSFET source).

logical_pins.name: FLTR
logical_pins.description: Connect a capacitor from FLTR to A to filter the input to the fast comparator.

logical_pins.name: BYP
logical_pins.description: Connect a storage capacitor from BYP to A to filter the gate drive supply voltage.

logical_pins.name: PG
logical_pins.description: Open-drain Power Good indicator. PG is open if UV/OV thresholds are met and internal UVLO is satisfied.
